$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every Insolvia design token. Stack-agnostic on purpose: `tool/generate_tokens.dart` renders it into Flutter Dart AND a Tailwind v4 @theme stylesheet. Never hand-edit a generated file — edit this and regenerate. Every token carries a `description`, which becomes the doc comment on the generated Dart member.

palette.$comment: Raw brand primitives. UI code must NOT read these directly — go through the semantic layer below so re-branding stays a one-file change. Placeholder professional-legal palette (brand not yet finalized — see O4): deep navy 'ink' for trust, warm brass for accent, paper neutrals.

inkBright.description: Deep navy, one step lighter — hover/pressed on ink surfaces.

inkDeep.description: Deep navy, near-black — the dark-mode canvas.

brassBright.description: Warm brass, lifted for legibility on dark surfaces.

paper.description: Neutral — warm off-white "paper"; the light-mode canvas.

linen.description: Neutral — one step down from paper, for inset/alt surfaces.

graphite.description: Neutral — near-black body text on light surfaces.

whiteAlpha20.description: White at 20% — hairline rules on dark surfaces.

spacing.$comment: A 4pt base grid. Use these instead of magic numbers so layout rhythm stays consistent across every Insolvia surface.

radii.$comment: Corner-radius scale for Insolvia surfaces and controls. Dart values are logical pixels; `css` is the authored rem/px equivalent.

md.description: 10 — buttons and the default control radius.

shadows.$comment: CSS-only. Flutter surfaces get elevation from Material's own system, so these are not mirrored into Dart.

sm.description: Hairline lift — hovered rows, sticky headers.

fonts.$comment: CSS-only. Flutter's type scale lives in `lib/src/tokens/typography.dart`; both stacks fall back to the platform UI family until the brand fonts land (O4).

heading.description: Display/heading family — apps may override to brand themselves.

body.description: Body/UI family — apps may override to brand themselves.

semantic.$comment: The indirection layer. Consumers brand themselves by overriding SEMANTIC tokens, never raw palette names. Each entry resolves a `{palette.*}` alias per theme; `light` is the @theme default and `dark` is emitted under [data-theme='dark'].

card.description: Raised surface behind cards, sheets, and dialogs.

surfaceAlt.description: Inset/alternate surface — table stripes, wells, code blocks.

brand.description: The brand ink used by headers and the wordmark.

primary.description: Primary action color (filled CTAs, focus, selection).

primaryText.description: Text/icon color that sits on top of [primary].

accent.description: Warm brass accent (highlights, focus, key CTAs).

semanticDerived.$comment: Hover/active states derived from a semantic token rather than pinned to a palette value, so overriding the semantic also moves its states. CSS emits `color-mix(in srgb, ...)`; Dart pre-computes the identical sRGB blend at generation time.
